data.content:
### User Logout (Bash)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

This snippet shows how to log a user out by revoking their refresh tokens. It includes options for logging out from the current session, all sessions globally, or all sessions except the current one.

```bash
# Logout from current session
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/logout' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'

# Response: 204 No Content

# Logout from all sessions (global scope)
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/logout?scope=global' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'

# Logout from all other sessions (keep current)
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/logout?scope=others' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'
```

--------------------------------

### User Logout

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

Logs out a user by revoking refresh tokens and optionally sessions.

```APIDOC
## POST /auth/v1/logout

### Description
Logs out a user by revoking refresh tokens. Can be used to revoke the current session, all sessions, or all sessions except the current one.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/auth/v1/logout`

### Query Parameters
- **scope** (string) - Optional - Determines the scope of the logout. Options: "global" (logout all sessions), "others" (logout all sessions except the current one). Defaults to revoking only the current session if not provided.

### Response
#### Success Response (204)
No Content.

### Request Example
```bash
# Logout from current session
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/logout' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'

# Logout from all sessions (global scope)
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/logout?scope=global' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'

# Logout from all other sessions (keep current)
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/logout?scope=others' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'
```
```

--------------------------------

### GET /verify - Verify Registration/Recovery/Magiclink via Query Params

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Verifies a registration, password recovery, or magic link via query parameters. The user is logged in and redirected to a specified URL with authentication tokens in the fragment. Used for client-side session handling.

```json
{
  "type": "signup",
  "token": "confirmation-code-delivered-in-email",
  "redirect_to": "https://supabase.io"
}
```

```url
SITE_URL/#access_token=jwt-token-representing-the-user&token_type=bearer&expires_in=3600&refresh_token=a-refresh-token&type=invite
```

--------------------------------

### Get User by ID (Admin) (Bash)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

This command demonstrates how to retrieve detailed information for a specific user by providing their unique user UUID. The response includes all user fields.

```bash
curl -X GET 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/admin/users/USER_UUID' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY'

# Response: User object with all fields
{
  "id": "user-uuid",
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "phone": "+1234567890",
  "user_metadata": {"full_name": "John Doe"},
  "app_metadata": {"provider": "email"},
  "identities": [...],
  "factors": [...],
  "created_at": "2023-09-15T19:53:12Z",
  "updated_at": "2023-09-20T10:00:00Z"
}
```

--------------------------------

### List Users (Admin) (Bash)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

This command shows how to retrieve a paginated list of all users within the system. It allows specifying the page number and the number of users per page for efficient retrieval.

```bash
# List users with pagination
curl -X GET 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/admin/users?page=1&per_page=50' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY'

# Response:
{
  "users": [
    {
      "id": "user-uuid-1",
      "email": "user1@example.com",
      "phone": "+1234567890",
      "created_at": "2023-09-15T19:53:12Z",
      "last_sign_in_at": "2023-09-20T10:00:00Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "user-uuid-2",
      "email": "user2@example.com",
      "created_at": "2023-09-16T12:30:00Z",
      "last_sign_in_at": "2023-09-19T14:20:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "aud": "authenticated"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Invite User (Admin) (Bash)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

This command illustrates how an administrator can invite a new user to the system via email. It also allows setting custom data for the user, such as their role and department.

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/invite' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "email": "newuser@example.com",
    "data": {
      "role": "admin",
      "department": "Engineering"
    }
  }'

# Response:
{
  "id": "user-uuid",
  "email": "newuser@example.com",
  "invited_at": "2023-09-20T16:00:00Z",
  "confirmation_sent_at": "2023-09-20T16:00:00Z",
  "created_at": "2023-09-20T16:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2023-09-20T16:00:00Z"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Unenroll MFA Factor (Bash)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

This command demonstrates how to remove a Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) method from a user's account using their factor UUID.

```bash
curl -X DELETE 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/factors/FACTOR_UUID' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'

# Response:
{
  "id": "factor-uuid"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Update Existing User (Admin)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

Updates an existing user's profile information, including email, user metadata, app metadata, and ban duration. Requires admin privileges and appropriate authentication headers.

```bash
curl -X PUT 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/admin/users/USER_UUID' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "email": "updated@example.com",
    "user_metadata": {
      "full_name": "Jane Smith Updated"
    },
    "app_metadata": {
      "role": "admin"
    },
    "ban_duration": "24h"
  }'
```

```bash
curl -X PUT 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/admin/users/USER_UUID' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "ban_duration": "none"
  }'
```

--------------------------------

### POST, PUT /admin/users/<user_id>

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Creates a new user (POST) or updates an existing user (PUT) identified by `user_id`. Supports setting roles, contact information, metadata, and ban duration.

```APIDOC
## POST, PUT /admin/users/<user_id>

### Description
Creates (POST) or Updates (PUT) the user based on the `user_id` specified. The `ban_duration` field accepts time units like "ns", "us", "ms", "s", "m", "h".

### Method
POST, PUT

### Endpoint
/admin/users/<user_id>

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **user_id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the user.

#### Query Parameters

#### Request Body
- **role** (string) - Required - The role to assign to the user.
- **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the user.
- **phone** (string) - Optional - The phone number of the user.
- **password** (string) - Optional - The password for the user (only required if type is 'signup').
- **email_confirm** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to confirm the user's email.
- **phone_confirm** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to confirm the user's phone number.
- **user_metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the user.
- **app_metadata** (object) - Optional - Application-specific metadata for the user.
- **ban_duration** (string) - Optional - Duration to ban the user (e.g., "24h"). Use "none" to unban.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "role": "test-user",
  "email": "email@example.com",
  "phone": "12345678",
  "password": "secret",
  "email_confirm": true,
  "phone_confirm": true,
  "user_metadata": {},
  "app_metadata": {},
  "ban_duration": "24h"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier of the user.
- **aud** (string) - The audience of the user.
- **role** (string) - The role assigned to the user.
- **email** (string) - The email address of the user.
- **phone** (string) - The phone number of the user.
- **email_confirmed_at** (string) - Timestamp when the email was confirmed.
- **phone_confirmed_at** (string) - Timestamp when the phone number was confirmed.
- **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user was created.
- **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user was last updated.
- **user_metadata** (object) - Custom metadata for the user.
- **app_metadata** (object) - Application-specific metadata for the user.
- **ban_duration** (string) - The duration the user is banned for, or null if not banned.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "d290f1ee-6c54-4b01-90e6-d701748f0851",
  "aud": "authenticated",
  "role": "test-user",
  "email": "email@example.com",
  "phone": "12345678",
  "email_confirmed_at": "2023-01-01T12:00:00Z",
  "phone_confirmed_at": null,
  "created_at": "2023-01-01T12:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2023-01-01T12:00:00Z",
  "user_metadata": {},
  "app_metadata": {},
  "ban_duration": null
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Enroll Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Factor

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

Enrolls a new multi-factor authentication method for the user. Supports TOTP, phone, and WebAuthn. Requires the project API key and an access token.

```bash
# Enroll TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password)
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/factors' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "factor_type": "totp",
    "friendly_name": "My Authenticator App",
    "issuer": "MyCompany"
  }'

# Enroll phone factor
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/factors' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "factor_type": "phone",
    "phone": "+1234567890",
    "friendly_name": "My Phone"
  }'

# Enroll WebAuthn (Passkey) - Step 1: Request challenge
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/factors' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "factor_type": "webauthn",
    "friendly_name": "My Security Key"
  }'
```

--------------------------------

### Create or Update User (Admin) (Bash)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

This command illustrates how an administrator can create a new user or update an existing user's details. It supports setting various user attributes like email, password, phone number, and metadata.

```bash
# Create new user (POST)
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/admin/users' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_ROLE_KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "email": "newuser@example.com",
    "password": "password123",
    "email_confirm": true,
    "phone": "+1234567890",
    "phone_confirm": true,
    "user_metadata": {
      "full_name": "Jane Smith"
    },
    "app_metadata": {
      "role": "premium"
    }
  }'
```

--------------------------------

### Challenge and Verify MFA Factor (Bash)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

This snippet demonstrates how to create a Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) challenge for verification and then how to verify the response using a provided code. It supports different channels like SMS.

```bash
# Step 1: Create challenge
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/factors/FACTOR_UUID/challenge' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{}'

# Response:
{
  "id": "challenge-uuid",
  "type": "totp",
  "expires_at": 1694808552
}

# For phone factor with SMS:
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/factors/FACTOR_UUID/challenge' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "channel": "sms"
  }'

# Step 2: Verify challenge with code
curl -X POST 'https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1/factors/FACTOR_UUID/verify' \
  -H 'apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "challenge_id": "challenge-uuid",
    "code": "123456"
  }'

# Response (upgraded to AAL2):
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "expires_in": 3600,
  "refresh_token": "new-refresh-token",
  "user": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "factors": [
      {
        "id": "factor-uuid",
        "status": "verified"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Invite User (Admin)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/auth/llms.txt

Invites a new user to the system via email, optionally with administrative privileges and custom data.

```APIDOC
## POST /auth/v1/invite

### Description
Invites a new user via email. This endpoint requires the `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/auth/v1/invite`

### Request Body
- **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the user to invite.
- **data** (object) - Optional - Custom data to associate with the user.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "email": "newuser@example.com",
  "data": {
    "role": "admin",
    "department": "Engineering"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - The UUID of the invited user.
- **email** (string) - The email address of the invited user.
- **invited_at** (string) - Timestamp when the invitation was sent.
- **confirmation_sent_at** (string) - Timestamp when the confirmation email was sent.
- **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user record was created.
- **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user record was last updated.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "user-uuid",
  "email": "newuser@example.com",
  "invited_at": "2023-09-20T16:00:00Z",
  "confirmation_sent_at": "2023-09-20T16:00:00Z",
  "created_at": "2023-09-20T16:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2023-09-20T16:00:00Z"
}
```
```
